summary: Testing QR Document Lookup system (doc code generation, PIN-protected lookup at /doc/:code) and PrintEngine v2 redesign integration with branch transfers. All features working correctly.

test_cases.detail: Returns 400 if doc_type or doc_id missing
Returns document details with branch transfer data
Document code CT7SP66X displayed with PIN input
Shows 'Invalid PIN' error for wrong PIN (000000)
PIN 521325 unlocks document, shows Branch Transfer details
From/To branches (Branch 1 → Branch 2), timeline, items table displayed
Print button available in document view
Generates document with Branch Transfer header
QR code included via qrserver.com API
Full page format with company info left, doc info right

branch_transfer_print_integration.detail: BranchTransferPage.js printTransferOrder function generates doc code via API and opens print window via PrintEngine

verified_features: POST /api/doc/generate-code returns unique 8-char alphanumeric code (auth required)
POST /api/doc/lookup with valid PIN returns document details (no auth required, PIN only)
/doc/:code route shows PIN gate with code display and PIN input
After valid PIN on /doc/:code, shows branch transfer with From/To branches, timeline, items
PrintEngine.js compiles and exports correctly with all document types
Branch transfer print button generates doc code and opens PrintEngine window with QR code
critical_code_review_comments: Console warnings about HTML nesting (<span> inside <tr>, <tr> inside <span>) in DocViewerPage TransferView - not blocking but should be cleaned up for better HTML semantics
seed_data_creation: None - used existing test doc code CT7SP66X
context_for_next_testing_agent: QR Document Lookup system fully tested. Test code CT7SP66X exists for branch_transfer doc type. PIN 521325 is valid manager PIN. DocViewerPage at /doc/:code is public route requiring only PIN, no login. PrintEngine generates professional full-page documents with QR codes. Console warns about HTML nesting in TransferView (span inside tr/tbody) - non-blocking.
rca_of_issues: Minor: DocViewerPage TransferView has HTML nesting warnings in console (span elements inside tr/tbody). This is from React's conditional rendering. Non-blocking but could be improved by using Fragment or proper table structure.
